sections.paragraph:
θρησκεύω (s. prec. entry; Hdt. et al.; SIG 783, 42f [27 b.c.] τοὺς θεοὺς ἐθρήσκευσεν εὐσεβῶς; Sb 991, 7 [III a.d.]; Herm. Wr. 12, 23 τοῦτον τὸν λόγον, ὦ τέκνον, προσκύνει καὶ θρήσκευε; Wsd 11:15; 14:17; TestJob 2:2; Just., A I, 62, 2) practice cultic rites, worship, w. the acc. of that which is worshiped or served (Dionys. Hal. 2, 23, 1 τοὺς θεούς; Celsus 8, 66; SIG [s. above]; Herm. Wr. [s. above]; Wsd 11:15; Jos., Ant. 8, 248; 9, 289; SibOr 5, 77; but cp. Theoph. Ant. 2, 35 [p. 186, 18] w. the dat., perh. influenced by the related terms προσκυνέω and λατρεύω) πῶς θρησκεύοντες αὐτόν (i.e. τ. θεόν) how you worship him Dg 1. τινά τινι serve or worship someone by means of someth. 2:8; θ. θρησκείαν engage in worship 1 Cl 45:7.—DELG.

prec. prec. = preceding

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

SIG SIG = Sylloge Inscriptionum Graecarum3; superscript omitted in text—List 3

Sb Sb = Sammelbuch griechischer Urkunden aus Aegypten—Lists 3, 4

Herm. Wr. Herm. Wr. = Hermetic Writings—List 5

TestJob TestJob = Testament of Job—List 2

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

acc. acc. = accusative

Celsus Celsus , in Origen, Contra Celsum, II a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

SibOr SibOr = Oracula Sibyllina, II–III a.d.—List 5

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Theoph. Ant. Theoph. Ant. = Theophilus Antiochenus, II a.d.—List 5

dat. dat. = dative

perh. perh. = perhaps

i.e.

i.e. = id est (that is)

I.-E. Indo-European

Dg Dg = Letter of Diognetus, attributed to an unidentifiable apologist; II a.d.—List 1

someth. someth. = something

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 459
